name: Snowfall spores
description:
Let it fall, let it tumble as it flutters through the breeze like snow, and as it piles higher and higher, watch us all else falls apart, that's the spores spread.

On the first day of Christmas, my true love gave to me a box full of plagues. On the second day of Christmas, my true love gave to me two twisted toys. On the third day of Christmas, my true love gave to me three correcting emotions. On the fourth day of Christmas, my true love gave to me Four Deadly shocks. On the 5th day of Christmas, my true love gave to me 5 monstrous beasts. On the 6th day of Christmas, my true love gave to me six carnivorous Snowmen. On the seventh day of Christmas, my true love gave to me 7 zomasaurs. On the 8th day of Christmas, my true love gave to me 8 copies of them. On the 9th day of Christmas, my true love gave to me nine bioweapons. On the 10th day of Christmas, my true love gave to me 10 snowy fungus.

Today, we have the snowfall spores infection. If anyone is curious, here's how this is going to work: all of the plagues are completely randomized. I have a spinner that has 100 options. Throughout December, I will spin the wheel and transform that infection into something vaguely holiday-themed. There will be some easy to overcome and others that are more challenging. Still, we'll see how this all goes I hope you'll join me for every one of them.

(The infection)

  1. Initial infection is caused by prolonged exposure to the spores, primarily through close contact with the fungus or close contact with an infected individual, especially one in later stages. Symptoms include coughing, sneezing, difficulty breathing, shortness of breath, shaky breath, scratchy throat, runny nose, possible dizziness, and brain fog. The best way to avoid infection is to avoid the spores as well as later-stage infected individuals who begin growing the fungus and producing the spores. Physical signs of infection may be discoloration near the injury, a slightly pale complexion, or visible spores on clothing or the body.

  2. Stage 2 begins 2 to 4 weeks after initial infection, when spores have spread through the body, primarily infecting the lungs, throat, and nasal passages. Symptoms include coughing, sneezing, scratching, throat difficulty, breathing difficulty, dizziness, loss of voice, foggy thoughts, difficulty focusing, hot flashes, cold spells, difficulty falling asleep, or remaining asleep. At this stage, infected individuals begin showing more outward signs of the infection, coughing out spores, and that's a fungus beginning to grow and build in their airways. Complexion becomes more pale white, and brown splotches appear across the body.

  3. Stage 3 begins 12 weeks after stage 2 spores have fully spread through the body fungus is not only growing internally, but externally, with a layer of fungus beginning to grow along the limbs. This stage is marked by complete loss of speech and focus. At this point, infected individuals are in a constant dazed state. The fungus grows all across the body, beginning to alter the limbs, becoming longer. Pain, along with dizziness, is caused by the spores and the transformation, leaving the infected individuals in a state of fuzziness and fogginess. The physical body begins growing and expanding as the fungus spreads, shifting and melding together, appearing like a thick layer of fur, with sharp points across the body.

  4. Stage 4 begins when she couldn't do weeks to a month after stage 3, varying mainly by temperature and whether or not the infected individual is exposed to even larger amounts of the spores. This stage is primarily marked by behavioral changes with infected individuals beginning to become aggressive, lashing out, and attempting to spread the infection, as well as seeking out othe
creator: CabbagePatchzombie
